title: ProvenanceManifest
$comment: Versioned sidecar manifest that makes a generated flow's BuildReport traceable to its inputs and execution environment. This is a SEPARATE format from the immutable docs/contracts/* schemas and never extends BuildReport. PRIVACY RULE (enforced by scripts/validate-provenance.mjs): the manifest MUST NOT contain credentials, secrets, tokens, API keys, environment-variable VALUES, raw prompts, user data, or model chain-of-thought/reasoning. Only content digests, coarse runtime identifiers, and bounded status codes are permitted. additionalProperties:false at every level keeps undeclared (and therefore un-reviewed) fields out.

provenanceVersion.$comment: Semantic version of THIS manifest format; consumers gate on the major.

createdAt.$comment: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when this manifest was generated.

commit.$comment: Full commit SHA, or the literal 'unavailable' when git metadata cannot be read. A missing repo must never fail a local run.

runtime.$comment: Coarse execution-environment identifiers. Deliberately excludes any environment-variable VALUES.

revision.$comment: Generator / instruction revisions, recorded only when supplied by the caller.

inputs.$comment: Content digests of every source artifact the BuildReport derives from. Digests are over CANONICAL JSON (recursively key-sorted, no insignificant whitespace) so they are stable across formatting, BOM, and CRLF/LF differences.

registryInventory.$comment: SELECTION-SCOPED digest: covers ONLY the registry items this flow references (screenPattern.registryItem + each block selection's registryItem, unioned over SelectionSpec.screens), NOT the whole registry/ directory. Computed as a key-sorted map of {referenced-item-name -> canonical digest of registry/<name>.json}, then digested. `items` records the sorted set of referenced names so the validator re-derives and recomputes the exact same set deterministically. Adding/editing/removing an unrelated registry item leaves this digest unchanged; changing a referenced item changes it.

items.$comment: Sorted, de-duplicated registry item names the digest covers.

durationMs.$comment: Wall-clock duration of the check/verification phase in milliseconds.

failure.$comment: null on success. On failure, a BOUNDED classification (never an opaque free-text-only field) plus a retry hint.

classification.$comment: code=source/logic defect; environment=host/runtime issue; dependency=external package/tool; policy=contract or governance rejection.

retryable.$comment: Whether an unchanged re-run could plausibly succeed (true for environment/dependency, usually false for code/policy).

summary.$comment: Optional short, non-sensitive human label. MUST NOT contain secrets, prompts, user data, or reasoning.

path.$comment: Basename of the source artifact, resolved location-independently via scripts/lib/paths.mjs.
